evals.name: midresource-yoruba-strategy
evals.prompt: I want to fine-tune an LLM for Yoruba. What's the right strategy?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) resolve Yoruba to ISO yor + Glottolog yoru1245, note tone-language status; (2) classify Joshi class (2 per the original 2020 paper, 3 per current FLORES-200/NLLB-200/MasakhaNER signals — either is acceptable so long as the class is named); (3) recommend Igbo/Hausa/Swahili as transfer sources (not English) with URIEL distances; (4) recommend vocab extension + LoRA + multilingual base (NLLB/AfroLM) over English fine-tune; (5) flag diacritic preservation as non-negotiable.

evals.name: class-0-disambiguation-quechua
evals.prompt: Train a Quechua model for me.
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) recognize 'Quechua' as a macrolanguage and STOP for disambiguation; (2) present Cusco/Ayacucho/Bolivian as options; (3) NOT proceed with strategy until disambiguation; (4) note community-engagement requirements (vitality + Indigenous data).

evals.name: class-3-swahili-multilingual
evals.prompt: We want to add Swahili support to our existing Llama 3 fine-tune. What should we know?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) resolve Swahili (swa); (2) Class 3 — has FLORES, NLLB, MasakhaNER; (3) note Latin script + Bantu noun classes; (4) URIEL transfer sources Zulu/Kinyarwanda/Xhosa; (5) recommend standard fine-tune + targeted MasakhaNER eval; (6) note Swahili is one of the better-resourced African languages — Class 3 is a real upgrade from Class 1-2 patterns.

evals.name: class-5-english-no-special-handling
evals.prompt: I'm training on English text. Anything special to know about scope?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) resolve English (eng); (2) Class 5 — no special low-resource heuristics needed; (3) standard pretrained tokenizers, full fine-tune, broad eval suites apply; (4) brief — don't over-engineer scope for a Class 5 language.

evals.name: polysynthetic-extreme-inuktitut
evals.prompt: I want to build something useful for Inuktitut speakers.
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) resolve Inuktitut (iku); (2) Class 0-1 — very limited data; (3) flag polysynthesis (tokenizer fertility 4-7×); (4) note URIEL distance to English ≥ 0.78 — English is hopeless as transfer source; (5) recommend West Greenlandic (kal) as closest source; (6) MANDATORY routing to magic-linguistic-ethics — Indigenous Canadian data; community engagement before any data acquisition; (7) suggest archive routes (DELAMAN/AILLA).
